channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday that he's picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions, tasking him with helping the incoming administration deal with some of the toughest foreign policy challenges.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East visit as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate first tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm has created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drones, planes or UFOs? Americans abuzz over mysterious New Jersey sightings
item.description: CHATHAM, N.J. (AP) — That buzzing coming out of New Jersey? It's unclear if it's drones or something else, but for sure the nighttime sightings are producing tons of talk, a raft of conspiracy theories and craned necks looking skyward.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas father set house on fire with 3 children inside: police
item.description: A Texas father is facing charges over allegations that he intentionally set his home on fire with his three children inside, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Detroit area's many Syrians are celebrating Assad's overthrow and planning long-delayed visits
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Nizam Abazid is gleefully planning his first trip in decades to Syria, where he grew up. Rama Alhoussaini was only 6 years old when her family moved to the U.S., but she's excited about the prospect of introducing her three kids to relatives they've never met in person.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting suspect fled New York via train, not by bus as originally thought: reports
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, the man accused of fatally shooting UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, is believed to have taken a train out of Manhattan, not a bus as originally thought.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas sues DuPont, 3M over 'forever chemicals' in consumer products
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) is suing companies that have historically made toxic “forever chemicals,” commonly referred to as PFAS, or products that contain them, alleging false advertising over their safety.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Bone-chilling cold returns to the Midwest in the wake of a storm that soaked the East Coast
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Frigid weather returned to the Upper Midwest on Thursday after a storm that swept up the East Coast delivered a blow to New England, packing powerful gusts that knocked out power along with a deluge of rain and warming temperatures that washed away snow and dampened ski resort
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Brown snow falls over Maine town; officials warn against touching, eating it
item.description: Residents of a Maine town are being asked to avoid touching or ingesting the “brown” show that fell in the area.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: The wife of a Wisconsin kayaker who faked his own death moves to end their marriage
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The wife of a Wisconsin kayaker who faked his own drowning so he could leave her and their children and meet a woman in Eastern Europe filed court documents Thursday seeking to end their marriage.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco names street for Associated Press photographer who captured the iconic Iwo Jima photo
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A photojournalist who captured one of the most enduring images of World War II — the U.S. Marines raising the flag on the Japanese island of Iwo Jima — will have a block in downtown San Francisco named for him Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Used this parking app? You may qualify for part of $32.8 million settlement
item.description: An estimated 21 million users of the ParkMobile parking app are said to have been impacted by the data breach.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: When the African Methodist Episcopal Church, arguably the world’s largest independent Black Protestant denomination, held its quadrennial General Conference in Ohio in August, among the agenda items was an issue that the Rev. Jennifer S. Leath had labored over for two decades: same-sex marriage.
item.dc:creator: DARREN SANDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI Director announces his resignation
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced he will resign before President-elect Donald Trump takes office next year and before the end of his 10-year-term.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and DAVID BAUDER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Secretly recorded videos are backbone of corruption trial for longest-serving legislative leader
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hours of secretly recorded videos and phone calls have offered a rare glimpse into how the longest-serving legislative leader in American history operated behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Belichick, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill finalize coaching deal: reports
item.description: Bill Belichick has reportedly been tapped as the next head coach for the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luxury real estate brokers charged in federal indictment with sex trafficking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother have been charged with luring, drugging and violently raping dozens of women over more than a decade, according to a federal indictment unsealed Wednesday in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
